Asa aimed to please God and sought to approve himself to him. It is fortunate that people walk by this rule: not to do what seems right in their own eyes or in the eyes of the world, but what pleases God. We find through experience that it is good to seek the Lord; it gives us rest. While we pursue the world, we encounter nothing but trouble. Asa consulted with his people about how to make good use of the peace they enjoyed and concluded with them that they must not be idle or complacent. A large army from Ethiopia invaded Asa’s kingdom. This hardship came upon them so that their faith in God might be tested. Asa's prayer is short, but it expresses genuine faith and expectation from God. When we go out in God’s name, we cannot fail to prosper, and all things work together for the good of those he favors.
